premise: Sector Rotation Momentum: buy stocks in sectors accelerating faster than the broad market (sectorBreadthAccelerating + sectorBreadthGreaterThanMarket) with emerging trend strength (adxRange 20-45, emaSpread). Differentiator vs VCP: active relative-strength sector rotation; no order-block alpha engine.

summary: No detectable edge: |meanLift|<clusteredSE at every horizon (5d 0.22 SE, 10d 0.40 SE, 20d 0.83 SE); 20d lift NEGATIVE (-0.076%). meanLift sign-flips across SPY down->up tertiles at 10d & 20d (down-regime-negative / up-regime-positive cancellation = narrow-leadership/mean-reversion failure mode). Firing ~25% with edge diffuse across ~3900 dates => genuine flatness, not lumpiness. Threshold sweep 2.7/3.0/3.3 clean & monotone but tuning a value statistically == 0; no ARS. Near-zero-lift universe filter, not a signal.

reason: Differentiator rejected at /condition-screen (design-time). Sector-rotation premise as constructed (binary accelerating+beating breadth gate) has no forward-return lift. NOT a firewall death — no config_hash marked DEAD, cross-candidate G13 brake NOT engaged.
next: If sector-rotation is pursued, it needs a STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT premise (cross-sectional relative-momentum rank, or sector leadership-transition anchoring) encoded as a NEW condition and re-screened from scratch — a fresh DRAFT, not a Fenrir parameter tweak.
